meta.reportLabel: Thu Report — PCE Day; UK services CPI re-accelerated to 3.7% (GBP re-armed), Brent broke to ~$73 (4-mo low)
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40
meta.regime: Dollar-strong, holding a 1-yr high (DXY ~101.6) with 2-3 Fed hikes priced. Two overnight shifts: UK services CPI re-accelerated to 3.7% (re-arming the BoE, firming GBP) and Brent collapsed ~5% to ~$73 (4-mo low), deepening CAD's drag. USD/JPY ~161.5 (near 1986 low); intervention coordination-flavoured but doubted. Everything funnels into today's US PCE.
meta.nextBigEvent: US PCE — Thu Jun 25, 08:30 EDT (the marquee test of Warsh's 'broader than energy' call)

dailyRead: The dollar-strong regime is intact and everything funnels into today's US PCE (08:30 EDT / 14:30 CET) — the one print that can fade a very crowded, 1-year-high dollar (DXY ~101.6), with markets now pricing 2-3 Fed hikes over the next year on Warsh's hawkish dots. Two things changed overnight: (1) UK services inflation re-accelerated hard — May services CPI jumped to 3.7% from 3.2% (core 2.6%), re-arming the BoE and reversing Wednesday's 'GBP cracking' read into a firmer GBP; and (2) Brent collapsed ~5% to ~$73 (WTI ~$70), a fresh 4-month low, deepening the drag on CAD and the tailwind for GER40. The cleanest trades remain the USD-divergence legs (EUR/USD short, EUR/JPY short), the oil/commodity split (USD/CAD long, AUD/CAD long), and the structural long-JPY core (USD/JPY short, JPYBASKET long) — but the JPY book is squeezed near a 1986 low (~161.5), so defend, don't add. A soft core PCE is the single event that bounces the whole crowded dollar book at once.

strength.drivers: Core PCE 3.3% (Apr); CPI 4.2%; claims ~229k. Fed 3.50-3.75%, hawkish hold, dots flip next move to a HIKE.
strength.forward: 🦅 Regime extending: median '26 dot ~3.8%, market prices 2-3 hikes/yr; DXY 1-yr high ~101.6. PCE TODAY the only near-term fade — a soft core bounces a crowded long.

strength.drivers: Core 2.6% (May, up); services CPI 3.7% (up from 3.2%, fastest in months); CPI 2.8% (held). BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold.
strength.forward: 🦅 Hawkish — services re-accelerated to 3.7%, shutting Wed's cut-tail and putting the rate story back. Risk: a soft activity wave re-splits it. Next BoE Aug.

strength.drivers: May CPI: headline 4.0%, trimmed mean 3.6%; unemp 4.5% (Apr). RBA 4.35%, hawkish hold, minutes 'risks to the upside'.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Sticky 3.6% trim arms an Aug-hike case, but risk-off + Fed repricing keep AUD/USD sub-0.70. A soft US PCE flips the USD leg and the firm CPI bites. Next RBA Aug.

strength.drivers: Core ~1.5%; PPI hot. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16), Summary of Opinions favours more hikes; spot ~161.5 (near 1986 low).
strength.forward: 🦅↔ Destination unchanged, spot squeezed. Katayama-Bessent reaffirmed US-Japan COORDINATION readiness, but markets doubt Tokyo's will after April's reserve-draining op — two-sided, the swing. Next BoJ late Jul.

strength.drivers: Headline easing; recovery establishing. RBNZ 2.25%; Q1 GDP firm +0.8% q/q. Markets price a 25bp hike Jul 9.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Firm Q1 GDP + a priced Jul RBNZ hike floor NZD; hawkish USD caps NZD/USD. Net firm-but-capped into Jul 9 RBNZ.

strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (gas +33.2%, food +3.8%); but trimmed core 2.0%, median 2.1%. BoC 2.25% (5th hold, Jun 10), looking through energy.
strength.forward: ⚖️🔻 Hot/broadening CPI gives CAD a floor; Brent's drop to ~$73 (4-mo low) re-presses the export side harder than Wed; BoC looks through energy. Next BoC late Jul.

strength.drivers: Core ~2.5% ('26 proj); headline 3.0% '26 proj; unemp ~6.3%. ECB 2.25% (hiked Jun 11), framed as one-off war insurance, not a cycle; growth cut to 0.8%.
strength.forward: ⚖️🔻 Pure USD-leg: hawkish Fed pushes EUR/USD down, dovish-frame ECB caps bounces. EZ PMI downturn easing is not a turn. Next ECB late Jul.

symbols.why: Cleanest macro divergence: hawkish-Fed (dots->hike, DXY 1-yr high, 2-3 hikes) vs an ECB that called its hike one-off war insurance.
symbols.risk: Crowded into PCE — a soft core TODAY bounces it; trail stops.

symbols.why: Weak yen HELPS this: structural BoJ-tightening destination + a capped, dovish-frame EUR — cleaner than the USD/JPY leg.
symbols.risk: A coordination-flavoured intervention spike snaps every XXX/JPY lower at once.

symbols.why: Brent's 5% drop to ~$73 (4-mo low) deepens CAD's export drag; CPI only a floor (core 2.0%) as BoC looks through. Stronger than Wed.
symbols.risk: A soft US PCE caps the USD leg; an oil re-spike rescues CAD.

symbols.why: Structural core (BoJ hiking, coord watch) but SQUEEZED ~161.5 (1986 low). Defend, do NOT add here.
symbols.risk: Tokyo silent + clean break = momentum higher; a JOINT intervention spike is the violent re-load.

symbols.risk: Risk-off hits AUD beta harder; a CAD-supportive oil bid.

symbols.why: Structural-JPY destination vs a CAD capped by 4-mo-low oil; firmer Canadian CPI only a mild headwind to the short.
symbols.risk: Oil/gold bid firms CAD; an intervention spike snaps it lower abruptly (the re-load).

symbols.why: USD headwind dominant: risk-off + Fed repricing keep it sub-0.70, overpowering the firm trim-mean CPI.
symbols.risk: The sticky 3.6% core + a soft US PCE flips the leg — the firm-AUD invalidation.

symbols.why: Two firm legs now: services CPI 3.7% re-armed a hawkish BoE vs a 1-yr-high dollar — no clean edge till PCE.
symbols.risk: Soft PCE lifts it; soft UK activity re-splits it.

symbols.why: Firm Q1 GDP + priced Jul RBNZ hike floor NZD; hawkish USD caps it — net neutral into Jul 9.
symbols.risk: Risk-on lifts NZD; a soft US PCE or hot data helps.

symbols.risk: Firm GBP + weak yen spot tilts it up; a coordinated intervention spike snaps it lower.

symbols.why: Weak yen leans it down, but risk-off carry unwind + RBA floor make it modest only.
symbols.risk: Risk-on carry holds it up; a coordinated spike is the abrupt re-load.

symbols.why: Structural JPY destination vs an NZD floored by firm GDP — carry offsets, low conviction.
symbols.risk: Risk-on carry dominates near-term -> stalls; an intervention spike re-loads.

symbols.risk: EZ vs Aussie data set direction.

symbols.why: ECB spent vs an NZD floored by firm GDP + Jul-hike pricing — slight NZD tilt.
symbols.risk: NZD risk beta or a late-Jul ECB shift.

symbols.why: Two firm legs: re-armed BoE (services 3.7%) vs hawkish-hold RBA (3.6% trim) — no clean edge.
symbols.risk: UK services vs Aussie sticky-core split sets direction.

symbols.why: Hawkish-re-armed GBP vs an NZD floored by firm GDP — both firm, no divergence.
symbols.risk: Lowest conviction; trade levels only.

symbols.why: Both commodity-bloc, both floored (RBA sticky-core vs RBNZ Jul-hike priced) — a wash.
symbols.risk: Aussie data vs RBNZ Jul 9.

symbols.why: Shifted from RANGE: firm NZD (GDP + Jul hike) vs a CAD pressed harder by Brent's drop to ~$73 — the oil move tilts it long.
symbols.risk: Oil-led disinflation undercuts NZD too; an oil bid lifts CAD.

symbols.why: The strong leg: 1-yr high ~101.6 on Fed-hike repricing (2-3 priced) + risk-off; EUR/JPY/GBP/CAD legs all soft vs USD.
symbols.risk: PCE TODAY is the swing — a soft core or risk-on wave fades a stretched, crowded long.

symbols.risk: Wait for a coordinated intervention or a PCE-led USD fade to re-load; thesis intact, timing wrong.

symbols.why: Bigger oil tailwind (Brent ~$73, 4-mo low) + EZ PMI downturn easing; hawkish-Fed rates the cap, cancelled talks/Lebanon widen the tail.

catalysts.note: Test of Warsh's 'broader than energy' call; median '26 core PCE ~3.3%. The dollar's only near-term fade — a soft core bounces EUR/USD, USD/CAD, AUD/USD at once.

catalysts.event: US final Q1 GDP; jobless claims

catalysts.note: 25bp hike priced; firm Q1 GDP backs it — the NZD swing.
The next AUD labour swing (no Aussie jobs today).
USD/JPY ~161.5; Katayama-Bessent reaffirmed coordinated-action readiness, though markets doubt follow-through — biggest near-term JPY-book swing.
Brent down ~5% to ~$73 on eased Hormuz flows + peace progress; stalled talks (Lebanon flare) keep a respike tail.
All next decisions parked to late July.

geopolitics: Flows are crushing the war premium. With Strait-of-Hormuz tanker traffic normalising and US-Iran peace progress holding, Brent fell ~5% to ~$73 and WTI to ~$70 on Wednesday — the lowest since late February and roughly 40% off the wartime peak. The diplomatic track is still stalled (the Burgenstock technical round remains cancelled amid the re-erupted Lebanon fighting), so the base case (truce holds, oil drifts lower) is intact, but the slipped talks keep the respike tail alive. Transmission: oil → energy/headline inflation → CB hawkishness → FX. Base case keeps the dollar bid, supports GER40 and risk-beta AUD/NZD on calmer days, and does NOT buy a Fed cut (Warsh marked inflation up into the oil drop); for CAD the hot CPI only partly offsets the deeper oil drag at ~$73 → soft-and-capped. Tail (alive): an MOU stall / Lebanon escalation re-spikes oil (rescues CAD, hurts AUD/NZD/GER40, hands USD & JPY competing haven bids) — the persistent ~$4,200 gold bid is the thin premium for exactly this.
